_README: Initial perf baselines for crossdesk microbench gates. Values in nanoseconds; pytest-benchmark JSON output keys these by test id. Update via dedicated PR; bench_check.py fails if a test runs >20% slower than the matching baseline. First wave: heartbeat FSM hot path. Real measurements replace these placeholders once the microbench CI job has run on a stable runner.

test_bench_pong_tick_healthy.comment: Single FSM.tick() with a healthy pong. Should stay sub-microsecond on commodity hardware; 5us baseline gives slack for slow CI runners until real numbers replace it.

test_bench_miss_tick.comment: Single FSM.tick() with a miss. Same shape as pong path.

test_bench_full_recovery_cycle.comment: 16 misses driving HEALTHY -> HARD_DESTROY. Aggregates per-tick cost; tighter than 16 * single-tick because the FSM short-circuits in HARD_DESTROY.
